channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: BOI electrifies 100 communities, connects 11,777 power users
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Bank of Industry (BOI) electrified over 100 communities and connected 11,777 new power users in 2025, highlighting its role in Nigeria’s energy access.
Read More: https://punchng.com/boi-electrifies-100-communities-connects-11777-power-users/

item.title: Vatican envoy meets Tinubu, hints at Pope’s Nigeria visit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Vatican envoy met President Tinubu, hinting at a future visit by Pope Leo XIV to Nigeria. Discover the details of this significant diplomatic engagement.

item.description: Fireworks lit up the New York City skyline as America marked its 250th anniversary.

item.title: Severe weather disrupts US’s 250th celebrations
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump says he will still speak in Washington, DC, after a thunderstorm delayed celebrations in the US capital.

item.title: Scorching Heat Forces Independence Day Events Cancellation Across US
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A severe heatwave sweeping across large parts of the United States disrupted Independence Day celebrations on Friday, forcing the cancellation of parades, temporary closure of public events and the treatment of several people for heat-related illnesses. item.description:

item.title: Wang Yi Says China Welcomes Stronger European Business Relations
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China welcomes deeper cooperation with Swedish and European businesses, which will benefit all parties involved, Foreign Minister Wang Yi said in a meeting with Investor AB Chairman Jacob Wallenberg in Stockholm on July 4, according to a statement from the ministry on Sunday.

item.title: NZ Pledges Trade Talks With Brazil, Europe Within Five Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Zealand’s governing National Party will begin negotiations for seven new trade deals within five years should it win the November election, Trade Minister Todd McClay said.

item.title: Investors Looking for Shelter From AI Storm Are Turning to India
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: After losing out big on the global AI rally, Indian equities are regaining the attention of investors seeking to weather the latest market turbulence.

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Operatives of the Nigeria Police Force have rescued an abducted woman and her two-year-old son from a forest hideout in Kwara State, in what authorities described as a successful intelligence-led operation against kidnapping syndicates. item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Vice President, Atiku Abubakar has expressed concern over the recent disclosure by the International Monitoring Fund ( IMF) that Nigeria omitted public expenditure equivalent to two percent of its Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the recent budget. item.description: The Police Command in Ekiti has rescued the victims abducted during an attack on the Christ Apostolic Church (CAC), Eda-Oniyo, on April 28 2026 . The post Ekiti: Eda Oniyo kidnap victims released after 67 days appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Zulum clears N5.81bn Gratuity Backlog for Retirees, Deceased Workers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Borno State Governor, Prof. Babagana Umara Zulum, has presented cheques worth N5.81 billion for the payment of outstanding gratuities to retired civil servants and the families of deceased workers. The governor presented the cheques on Friday at the Council Chamber of the Government House, Maiduguri

item.description: President Bola Tinubu has flagged-off a N137 billion Rehabilitation work on the Bama–Banki Road and the Dikwa–Gamboru–Ngala Road in Borno. The project area lies in the Northeastern part of Nigeria, in Borno, and borders the neighbouring Republic of Cameroon. The existing road is a single carriage of

item.title: Morocco beat Canada to reach consecutive World Cup quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Morocco booked a place in the quarter-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after defeating co-hosts Canada 3-0 in their Round of 16 clash at the Houston Stadium. The African champions sealed victory with two second-half goals from Azzedine Ounahi—his first-ever FIFA World Cup goals—and a stoppage-time

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust

item.description: World Cup favourites France book their quarter-final place thanks to Kylian Mbappe's second-half penalty against a stubborn Paraguay side in Philadelphia.

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A former Gombe State Commissioner of Information and Culture, Hon. Ibrahim El-Hassan Kwami is dead. He died Saturday in Gombe after a brief illness. His remains were buried after a funeral prayer, which held at the Gombe Emir’s Palace Jumu’at Mosque. The deceased served as Commissioner of Info

item.description: South Africa has ruled out any compensation for Nigerians who left properties behind after being evacuated from the country. item.dc:creator: PM News

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the consulate, Musa Joe was allegedly killed by suspected criminals in Witbank, Mpumalanga Province, while Emeka Iroegbu reportedly died after being tortured by officers of the Tshwane Metro Police in Sunnyside, Pretoria.

item.title: Fintiri Recommits to Delivering Legacy Projects in Adamawa
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Governor Ahmadu Fintiri has reaffirmed his administration's commitment to completing key infrastructure and education projects before leaving office, declaring that Adamawa will hand over a stable state equipped with institutions and road networks designed to drive economic growth, skills developmen

item.title: Scotland make impressive start in statement away win over Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scotland banished any lingering demons from November as they kicked off their Nations Championship with an impressive 47-38 away win over Argentina.

item.title: Coach Renard leaves Tunisia after World Cup group-stage exit
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Renard had been appointed by the federation on June 16 to succeed Lamouchi, who was sacked after Tunisia's 5–1 thrashing by Sweden in their opening match. The post Coach Renard leaves Tunisia after World Cup group-stage exit appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: James doubtful for Mexico tie but Quansah fit again
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Reece James misses training on the eve of England's World Cup last-16 tie against Mexico but Jarell Quansah is available again.

item.title: Kebbi Gov Idris Inaugurates New Governing Board for State Varsity
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Kebbi State Governor, Nasir Idris, has inaugurated a new Governing Board for Abdullahi Fodio University of Science and Technology, Aliero (AFUSTA), Following the dissolvment of the former Governing Council and removal of the Vice-Chancellor as well as two Deputy Vice-Chancellors following a prol

item.title: US influence challenged by changing global order: Paolo von Schirach
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US influence challenged by changing global order: Paolo von Schirach

item.title: Igboho’s ‘Iru Ekun’ security rescues abducted Oyo woman, son in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The founder of the Iru Ekun Security Network and Yoruba Nation activist, Chief Sunday Adeyemo, popularly known as Sunday Igboho, has announced the rescue of a woman and her son who were abducted in Igbope, Oorelope Local Government Area of Oyo State. The post Igboho’s ‘Iru Ekun’ se

item.title: ‘I look forward to hosting Pope Leo’ – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President Tinubu said interfaith dialogue is the only path to addressing the country's security challenges The post ‘I look forward to hosting Pope Leo’ – Tinubu appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Nigeria Will Continue To Partner With Religious Leaders To Promote Peace, Tolerance, Says Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The ex-Lagos governor said interfaith dialogue is the only path to addressing the country's security challenges. The post Nigeria Will Continue To Partner With Religious Leaders To Promote Peace, Tolerance, Says Tinubu appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Saraki steps in to sponsor First-Class UNILORIN graduate’s journey to Italy
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Adisa, who graduated with a First Class in Mathematics with a CGPA of 4.76/5.0 from UNILORIN, had disclosed that she had secured a fully funded Master's scholarship in Mathematics at the University of Calabria, Italy, but required financial assistance to cover the cost of relocating.

item.title: Nigeria’s Young Mathematicians Just Made History at the 2026 Pan-African Mathematics Olympiad
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Team Nigeria has returned from the 2026 Pan-African Mathematics Olympiad in Yamoussoukro, Ivory Coast, with three silver medals and one bronze, including a historic double-silver podium finish by two Nigerian female students. The post Nigeria’s Young Mathematicians Just Made History at the 202

item.title: Premier League quartet want Scott - Sunday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Bournemouth's Alex Scott wanted by Premier League quartet, Arsenal agree to sell Leandro Trossard, Manchester United's Kobbie Mainoo on Real Madrid's radar, plus more.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Codex reasoning-token clustering may be leading to degraded performance
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/30364 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48789428 Points: 179 # Comments: 56
